summary: DHH announces that Signal v Noise, Basecamp's long-running blog, is leaving Medium after three years and returning to self-hosted WordPress. He explains that Medium's shift toward a paid membership/aggregation model no longer aligns with their goals of writing to share ideas rather than monetize content. He frames the move as part of a broader trend away from centralized platforms and back toward independent blogs. The new site also aims to reduce their dependency on Twitter by becoming the primary home for all their thoughts.
key_points: Medium's pivot from attracting publishers to selling memberships changed the value proposition for Signal v Noise
Centralization of the internet through platforms like Medium carries real costs that are becoming increasingly apparent
Traditional independent blogs are poised to come back as people recognize the downsides of platform dependency
The relationship with Medium shifted from mutual benefit to one-sided — SvN was doing more for Medium than Medium was doing for them
Owning your own domain and platform is essential for long-term independence
money_quotes: Writing for us is not a business, in any direct sense of the word. We write because we have something to say, not to make money off page views, advertisements, or subscriptions.
We've grown ever more aware of the problems with centralizing the internet.

Traditional blogs might have swung out of favor, as we all discovered the benefits of social media and aggregating platforms, but we think they're about to swing back in style, as we all discover the real costs and problems brought by such centralization.
These days there's less of a 'what Medium is doing for us', and a whole lot more of 'what we're doing for Medium'.
The intention is to lessen our dependency on Twitter too, and simply turn Signal v Noise into the independent home for all our thoughts and ideas — big or small.
key_insight: Platform centralization inevitably shifts the value exchange against content creators, making independent publishing the only sustainable long-term strategy.
